what_it_does: This file drafts the commit grammar for the channel-memory vault: the rules for writing to the shared memory, including directory layout, required provenance headers, authorship norms, data-format preference, and a Tim-gated execution boundary.
observations: Status is DRAFT v0.1, framed by the lead, intended to be co-designed and not locked in one session.
The vault lives at ~/company/channel-memory/ and is committed to the ~/company git repo.
Layout is split into vision/, schema/, scans/, map/, plus this COMMIT-GRAMMAR.md file.
Every entry must carry a provenance header with one of three trust tags: tim-direct, channel-relayed, or fabric-derived, plus author, date, and verified fields.
tim-direct requires a structural test: no <channel> wrapper and a harness 'Message sent at…' marker; the receiving session id must be recorded.
A peer's tim-direct in another session is only a proposal to a consuming session, because cross-instance agreement is not independent confirmation.
Shared docs require a channel proposal and another member's ack; members may freely add files and edit their own entries.
Scans and maps must be committed as structured data (json/ndjson) so the Company UI can project them.
Committing a gated plan is not execution; [TIM-GATED] actions wait for Tim's direct go, while additive/read-only work is autonomous.
The grammar is intentionally minimal and will harden only after Tim confirms the program it serves.
standouts: The trust-tag provenance model and the rule that channel-relayed information is a proposal, not a mandate.
The structural test for tim-direct messages and the per-session nature of confirmation.
The explicit separation between committing a plan and executing [TIM-GATED] actions.
conventions: Markdown with numbered sections and a status/version header.
Metadata convention of trust/author/date/verified on every entry or row.
Directory convention separating source-of-truth vision, schemas, raw scan data, and lineage maps.
concerns: The structural test for tim-direct is described informally and may be hard to enforce automatically.
The verified field values (by-use / read-only / unconfirmed) are not defined.
No concrete filename schema, link convention, or manifest is provided yet.
Because it is a draft, the rules are not yet binding.
notes: A draft social-technical contract for channel-memory writes, centered on provenance tags and a Tim-gated execution boundary.
questions: What exactly is a session id and how is it formatted?
How is the harness 'Message sent at…' marker represented inside stored files?
What is the Company UI / Heart projection mechanism referenced for structured data?
Who qualifies as a member and how is membership or an ack recorded?
What is the precise scope of [TIM-GATED] actions beyond the examples given?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes: it states it is a commit grammar for provenance-trusted writes and then defines exactly those rules.
intention_signals: Establish a lightweight, trust-aware writing discipline so that relayed model outputs do not harden into false certainty, while keeping execution decisions under Tim's direct control.
novelty: Explicitly treats same-model peer relay as a proposal rather than confirmation, and uses trust tags as load-bearing provenance metadata at file or row level.
contribution: Provides a provenance and governance convention set for a shared memory vault, including trust tags, authorship rules, structured-data guidance, and a Tim-gated execution boundary.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a draft commit grammar for the channel-memory vault that mandates provenance trust tags, directory layout, shared-edit norms, structured-data preference, and Tim-gated execution.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to prevent relayed model outputs from being mistaken for confirmed intent by making provenance explicit and keeping risky execution decisions gated by Tim.
